summary: Iteration_4 frontend re-test after main agent added 4 missing handlers (saveMega, saveInhab, deleteInhab, toggleDistrict, reloadInhabs) in AvocatSheet.jsx. CRITICAL BUG FROM ITER_3 IS FIXED: AvocatSheet now renders all 5 tabs without ReferenceError. Méga tab fully functional end-to-end (district toggle + art486 + save → success toast 'Profil Méga enregistré' confirmed). However, saveMega calls onSaved?.() which CLOSES the sheet immediately after a Méga save, breaking the in-sheet workflow and preventing testing of Inhabilité/Web/Adresses tabs in the same session.

ui_bugs.issue: saveMega() calls onSaved?.() which the parent (Avocats.jsx) interprets as a full save — sheet closes after Méga save. Tab-level saves should NOT close the sheet; only persist + show toast. Should call a lighter callback like onUpdated?.() or simply refresh the avocat in place.

integration_issues.issue: After saving Méga, sheet closes. User cannot continue editing Inhabilité/Web/Adresses without re-opening avocat. This breaks expected GestionCardex VB workflow where one fiche stays open across tabs.

verified_working: Login admin@gestioncardex.qc / Admin2026! → redirects to dashboard
Avocats list page renders, 1 seeded avocat (AV001 Tremblay Marie) visible
Open existing avocat → AvocatSheet renders WITHOUT ReferenceError (iter_3 critical bug FIXED)
All 5 tabs visible: tab-ident, tab-adr, tab-inhab, tab-mega, tab-web
Méga tab: mega-district-1 checkbox clickable, mega-art486 toggle clickable
save-mega-btn click → PUT /api/avocats/{id}/mega succeeds → green toast 'Profil Méga enregistré'
No console errors, no runtime overlay during AvocatSheet render
not_tested_this_iteration: Inhabilité tab add/save (blocked by sheet auto-close after Méga save in same session — needs separate session)
action_items: Remove or change the onSaved?.() call inside saveMega/saveInhab/saveWeb in AvocatSheet.jsx — those tab-level saves should NOT close the sheet. Either omit the callback entirely after these per-tab persistences, or introduce a non-closing onUpdated?.() prop and call that instead. The handleSubmit (Identification tab) is the only handler that should trigger onSaved (to close+refresh list).

After fix, re-run full test of Inhabilité (add period, date début, save), Web (codeusager + password 6+ chars, save), Adresses (add adresse, mark courant), Rapports (6 download buttons present + clickable), Utilisateurs (create editeur, role badge), and brute-force lockout (5 fails → 429 on test account brute-test@x.com).
critical_code_review_comments: AvocatSheet.jsx is now ~524 lines with all 5 tabs in one file. Recommend splitting into AvocatSheetIdent, AvocatSheetAdresses, AvocatSheetInhab, AvocatSheetMega, AvocatSheetWeb sub-components.

All four save handlers (saveAdresse, saveInhab, saveMega, saveWeb) follow nearly identical try/catch+toast+reload pattern — extract a shared useApiSave(url, payload, successMsg) hook to reduce duplication.
No error boundary wraps the sheet — a future undefined ref would still tear down the entire sheet content. Wrap with <ErrorBoundary>.
saveMega/saveInhab use api.put with object as second arg but no explicit content-type — works because axios defaults to JSON, but be explicit for maintainability.
ESLint passes despite previous undefined refs because no-undef is not catching JSX handler references — tighten with eslint-plugin-react/jsx-no-undef rule.

success_rate.frontend: Méga tab + sheet render 100%; remaining 4 tabs + Rapports + Utilisateurs + brute-force = blocked/not retested

seed_data_creation: Reset users failed_logins=0 and lockout_until=null in MongoDB before testing. No new seed data created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter_3's critical 4 ReferenceErrors are FIXED — AvocatSheet renders 5 tabs and Méga tab fully works. New blocker: saveMega/saveInhab/saveWeb call onSaved?.() which closes the sheet. Once main agent removes onSaved?.() from non-Identification tab saves (or introduces onUpdated?.()), re-run full sweep: open avocat → switch through all 5 tabs in one session → Rapports → Utilisateurs CRUD → brute-force /api/auth/login with brute-test@x.com (5 fails should yield 429). DB lockout reset cmd: python3 -c "from pymongo import MongoClient; import os; MongoClient(os.environ.get('MONGO_URL'))['gestion_cardex']['users'].update_many({},{'$set':{'failed_logins':0,'lockout_until':None}})"
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause of iter_3 ReferenceErrors: handler functions referenced in JSX onClick/onChange were never defined. Main agent's fix (mcp_insert_text after deleteAdresse, lines 138-176) defines reloadInhabs, saveInhab, deleteInhab, saveMega, toggleDistrict using the standard try/api/toast/reload pattern. Verified live: opening avocat AV001 no longer crashes; Méga district toggle + art486 + save → 200 OK + toast. New issue surfaced: saveMega's onSaved?.() side-effect collapses the sheet, indicating onSaved was overloaded for both 'create avocat' (closes) and 'tab save' (should stay open). Mitigation: split callback contract into onCreated (closes sheet) and onUpdated (refreshes parent without closing).
